reviews.review_text: The guy who owns the caravan park was bashing on my window so hard and swearing I thought he was gonna to break my car What a joke don’t go there wangi wangi caravan park avoid everywhere else is beautiful besides the angry man at the caravan park nearly broke my car the owner

Been here a few times, both in a cabin & our caravan, love it, easy to access, chill enough, lake & pontoon nearby, large clean spacious toilet/shower block (bring own shower toiletries) the check in office has a couple of snacks/icecreams, & great recommendations of where to go for what your after to see/do.Undercover camp kitchen with a fridge! Not much is nearby, 15 mins away for most main shops. There was couple of small ones a few mins down the road.
Cons/ early morning the wild birds can be very noisy for light sleepers, even though I love them and the ducks 🦆 but it’s a wake up call & to go checkout the sunrise on the pontoon!

Spent the night there recently, beautiful location, right on the lake with great fishing. Very clean toilets and shower block and good bbq area and picnic tables but a washing up area for cleaning up cookware would be a very welcome addition.
This place was not the best abit dirty but a good pace to stay in caravan or unit
Wonderful place to spent holiday, , facilities are well organised and clean.

I went up to spend some family time with my son and had an awesome stay the cabin accommodation was excellent along with the friendly staff who went out of there way to make my stay great the area and the lake are truly beautiful and we had a great time fishing I will definitely be returning.
Good point for sailing and picnic.
Jo was an absolute god-send. She was friendly, helpful and knows her stuff! Absolute delight and I really appreciated all her help. What a beautiful atmosphere she creates
Loved ever thing about it great spot to go lots of thing for kids
Local muito bonito e espaçoso. Um lago maravilhoso com uma vista linda do nascer do sol. Ótimo para quem busca descanso.

Manager Jo and daughter who works in reception are The WORST customer service I have EVER experienced in a holiday/caravan park and I have done a lap of the country staying at over 100 holiday parks. I booked two sites 6 weeks ago under the impression from the website that each site holds 6 people 1 car per site. At no point during the booking process was I asked to add guests. If guest numbers affected pricing, the system shouldn't have allowed the booking to be completed without that step. That would be a booking system issue rather than a customer error. Especially on NYE one of the busiest peak times.
I tried explaining this  to Jo and her daughter working in the reception and explained how I was not privy to the information and how other guest may be confused also when not aware of this clause which is not stated anywhere in the booking and cancellation policy.
Jo was so difficult to deal with and having her daughter in the reception at the time seemed like a conflict of interest to me as the daughter initially seemed to understand my point of the booking issue and even said her self on her end my booking said 6 people on each site as Jo was initially busy when I arrived into the office to discuss this with her. Jo came soon after and that’s when I felt very uncomfortable and misunderstood.

Once I left the office I headed out of the campsite to grab some things for our stay.

When I returned back my guest told me the manger Jo had come up looking for me as she was going to have staff at another park fired due to me telling her I have stayed in the sister park many times and not had this booking issue. Jo for some reason took this as her job to fire staff from other facility’s. She came to my site while on the phone to the other facility telling my guest “where is Kodie, when did she stay at Swansea park because I’m going to get whoever let her stay there fired”
I learned this information when I returned back to the camp site and found a random key on they floor I asked who it belongs too and my guest said it fell out of Jo’s pocket when she came up here looking for me she tripped over one of our swag ropes, I believe it is a cabin key but we didn’t feel comfortable going to the office returning it as Jo intimidated me and my guests.
There was also a domestic violence issue with some other patrons of the park at 4.30am on new years morning where the entire park was woken up to fighting and the police arriving some guest were quite traumatised.
I expected some duty of care from park staff/ manager to check on guests the next day as it was a very intense situation for others in the park including my group who were camped beside. Not once did management check on people around the incident and I could not contact the office during the emergency as the phone wouldn’t connect, 3 people in the park called the police that I heard and no check ins from anyone working or associated with the park the next day. I am SO disappointed in this park and especially management. The lack of information provided when we asked for more than just a verbal policy off Jo was terrible Jo had no time to explain politely and just kept repeating PAY OR LEAVE. Not understanding we were happy to pay the entire time we were just confused on the online booking process and were trying to rectify it with management.
Absolutely top 5 favourite parks. Cleanest amenities in NSW. Amazing staff. Will definitely stay again.

A magical spot nestled amongst a serene outlook across port Mac. Opposite the sand islands. Plenty to do and close to shops, RSL and restraints if needed. Jo is a star at reception effortlessly solving problems and answering enquiries. We had to relocate last minute from the nth bush fires recently and she made it smooth as butter.
Lovely park. Easy parking for van and car. Clean and tidy facilities. Obvious upgrades occurring but was no bother. Beautiful views. Nice walks around Park area.
Feels like home
The lady working today (I presume Jo) was incredibly rude. By far the rudest person I have met all year at any restaurant, cafe, or any other customer related service.
Good and affordable camp ground
What a hidden gem. We will certainly be back.
Excellent gem of a caravan park. Great Lakes views.

Fantastic well run CVP.
Very clean amenities.
10 stars for Matt, Jo and all the staff.
Beautiful little spot that was spoilt by the scruffy residence vans/homes crap laying every where very disappointed
Tell nobody about this place. Too good to share
Picnic grounds were great

The location is breathtaking by the lake with a boat ramp . The positives it’s clean and a great view .
The ducks gave us a very warm welcome
The negative . I was unwell so requested an ensuite … wow come on $21 plus for one night . Have travelled 5 states and territories and never paid more than $12 before .
Seems a bit heavy .
The only other thing is the lower powered are near a road and even after midnight cars constantly using it for fishing and boating .
Great place to stay for a few nights. Very neat and tidy with clean facilities.

We had an extremely disappointing experience at this caravan park and would not recommend it to anyone. The noise levels were unbearable, with loud disturbances continuing well into the night, making it impossible to get any rest. The customer service was shockingly rude—staff were dismissive and unhelpful when we raised concerns. To top it all off, one of our personal items was stolen during our stay, and the management showed no interest in addressing the issue. Overall, this was a frustrating and unpleasant experience that we won’t repeat.

We had every bad experience when stay over Easter holiday. The local drunken youth hang around the park very late and noisy. Campsites were flooded and muddy. Not recommend for family camping people like us.
Very  friendly  clean nice people  meat  some nice helpful  people  as was our first time in our new second hand  caravan  than you
Had a lovely weekend. Cabins are great & the place was great for our boat.
Great spot, great amenities including a ce, ommunity kitchen with microwave and dishwater, hot and cold water, beautiful  scenery

2nd visit. Snagged a high site out running Alfred cyclone. Next to NP. Even though weather not ideal, still a nice spot. Local eateries, petrol station…..didn’t get a chance to do reserve walk along foreshore this visit. We will return.
Great spot on the lake close to a large bush reserve
Need a change in management there all rude and disrespectful specially the women that runs it

What a piece of heaven!
Facilities clean and function well,  cabin was perfect,  well stocked with everything needed for a comfortable stay.  Bird life plentiful and friendly.  Staff beautiful.  Local businesses are great,
RSL food awesome locals friendly.  I Highly recommend staying here we will be back because 2 nights was not long enough

What a beautiful spot to pop away for the weekend.
Managers are just lovely and couldn’t be more helpful
We will be back 🥰

This was actually… better than I expected. The facilities were very clean, like the toilet and laundry. And the staff were very polite. Can you get any better? The only thing that put me off was the rain, but it wasn’t their right? RIGHT?! But I think that the best part was that there is direct access to the beach, and it had its own pier. You can also fish on it, so yes, you can bring those fishing rods :)

We only came here to join friends for a picnic at the foreshore but we also strolled around the van park grounds. The van park has location in spades being right on the shore of Lake Macquarie, it's got amazing views from most sites and great access to the water for swimming, fishing, kayaking. Roads in the park are tight but worth the extra manoeuvring for the view.
Stunning location

Fabulous location. Park is very old looking with a few long termers scattered around the place. Amenities clean. Fees are over the top for one person $54 per night powered for a slab & a piece of dirt. Very untidy permanent vans around the entrance.
I got a powered camp sites  and slept  in my rooftop tent.

Beautiful location.  The most scenic spots are the unpowered sites. But they are all good. Great walks and bike riding trails. Plenty of places to have a picnic or BBQ.  Shops are near by, I can recommend the fish and chip shop. Best battered fish I've ever had.  RSL good location on a Sunday afternoon 👌 😀
After recovery from surgery, looked after really well.

What a delightful place on the beautiful lake! The manager was very welcoming and helpful. Some tight spots to park a caravan but next time we’ll ask for easier access. Views from all sights. Beautiful bush walk from the park.

We have our own van and stay at the park regularly. It is a beautiful park right on the point at Wangi. It's close to the shops and eateries. The grounds are well set out with gardens, fire pits, BBQ area and playground for the kids. There's also a jetty. Huge trees offer shade. Jo and Matt are friendly and knowledgeable on the area. It's a fantastic place to stay.

Loved the location (we are locals) but the management in the office were extremely rude, and for the price the facilities are outdated and are timed and have set temperatures. We originally booked a non-powered for three nights, at $40 per night (only an additional $6/night for a powered site, however our car has a battery system that we used to keep us going for the duration of the stay), we decided to stay longer. Upon going in to pay, she very rudely accused us of speeding the previous night when she heard a V8 and she thought as we had one it was us, though there were numerous others (we hadn’t even driven our car the previous day). When I explained this to her, she was very rude annd abrupt. The next issue we had was I had extended a further two nights and paid one night on card and the second in cash. On what we hoped was our last day, wanting to extend one more night, we had quickly used a power lead to a caravan site just behind us to charge my laptop so when we returned from school drop off I’d have a charged laptop so I could work (I didn’t want to take it over to the camp kitchen and leave my laptop there). The lead ran right next to a car, and we’d spoken with the people on that site. On our way back, we had an extremely rude phone call advising we were to vacate the site immediately as we were using power and the ground men could have died as he didn’t see it to the last minute whilst mowing. Although I totally understand, we made sure the lead was close to other vehicles, it was bright orange and had been on for max half an hour. We were evicted from the site, and she was EXTREMELY rude over the phone and wouldn’t allow us to explain. She said we were on a non powered site and we were not permitted to use any electricity (although it was an additional $6 per day), and I would have used the same amount of electricity in the camp kitchen. Upon hanging up, I listened to a voicemail where she said the grounds man had noticed it before he had started mowing, so she lied on the phone. She also double charged us, without prior consent, for a night we had already paid for (refunded when we told her this, she did fight back but we had receipts to prove it). The matter could have been handled much much better than it was, and had we had a nice discussion, we would have stayed longer and left with the intent to return. Very disappointing, from someone who has worked in a customer facing role, that we were treated the way we were and things were handled the way they were. We were intending to book an extended stay once we moved from our home until we left for our travels around Aus; but we won’t be returning again.
Lovely spot it would be nice to be allowed to bring our furbaby
Management was shocking, location is nice.
Great place for a weekend away.

I parked my caravan for less than 1 week, until a girl was screaming at the top of her lungs like she was being injured. I spoke with the lady in the morning & she was rude & I told her someone tampered with my caravan and she became even ruder. The lady then made a mark on my name & now I'm NOT allowed in any caravan park under lake Macquarie.
I literally live in the gutter and I hope and pray I'm not attacked or worse.
The lady has given me a lifetime ban and my complaint disregarded.
I hope I don't become another statistic in Australia because I made a complaint and the lady is on a power trip playing god who can stay in a caravan park and who can live in the gutter.

Great location with powered pitches only metres from Lake Maquarie. Facilities very nice with timed and pre set temperature showers. Courtesy bus to the RSL club available with a $2 per person return fee. Very quiet at night 😴. Sites have communal fire pits which is nice and the Possums come out at night to say hello! 🦦. Only downside for us was the price of $60 per night which is a bit pricey.

What a beautiful spot topped off by awesome staff. We only stayed two nights but we will be back. It is a peaceful park with lots of birdlife to take in. Our RV was across from the lake and had fantastic views. The lady at the desk was very accomodating and pleasant and helped us with every request we made. We had a few issues in the stay that were our fault but she willingly helped with solutions. Relaxing with that view easy. Facilities were very clean and adequate. Thanks for a great few days.

Beautiful holiday park. But you must respect the land you walk on as Awabakal council managed. Strick rules to protect and please don't feed the animals. Only the correct food can be used but not incourage. This place is a beautiful spot to visit to see the best sunrise and sunsets. Not a permanent residency. Just to be enjoyed for holiday purposes only. Or the perfect short term home office.
Over $700 a week for a van with no bathroom, way to take advantage of the rental crisis
Nice
Looks a nice spot

What a beautiful, beautiful spot! A couple of metres from the water's edge lays this practical though a little dated caravan and camping park. Toilets have been renovated and look and function well. Boat ramp just metres away. Peaceful location. Happy customers.
Great place for a picnic bush walk or a mountain bike ride

Wat een super leuke eigenaren Matt en Jo. Wat hebben we hier 2 dagen genoten van de natuur. Vogels die gezellig een appel komen eten. Goede vis tips gekregen. En een middag gevist met een goede vangst. Heel gastvrij en een super service. Omgeving is ook weer betoverend!!

Nice caravan and unit park at the end of the Wangi peninsula.  It’s a bit remote but in a good location.  Be aware there are camping/ van sites available in the bush just past the main campground that are in a great location and I’m surprised I wasn’t offered one of those instead of the boring slab sites.  Btw, it’s pronounced ‘wun-gee’, not ‘Wang-e’ apparently.  Who knew.
Great little park on the shores of Wangi, beautiful spot !
